rationale: The model recommended medium-roast drip coffee based on the earlier session, but the user's most recent morning beverage (session s3) is ceremonial-grade matcha, making the response based on an outdated version of the user's preference.

The response suggests a generic Quinoa and Black Bean Bowl without referencing the user's established plant-based proteins (tempeh or tofu) from the most recent session, nor does it reference the earlier Mediterranean grain bowl with grilled chicken; it avoids the target protein slot entirely.
The response suggests pad see ew or pad thai from Thai Orchid, which is the outdated Friday night order; the active version is Vietnamese (pho or bun cha) as established in session s4.

The model recommended a 60-minute vinyasa flow class, which is the outdated version of the user's yoga preference. The active version, based on session s3, indicates the user has shifted to 75-minute yin classes with long-held poses.

The model scheduled a three-day push/pull/legs split, which is the outdated version of the user's training setup; the active version is a four-day upper/lower split as implied by the user's later references to two upper and two lower sessions.

The response follows the outdated bedtime routine (bath, two stories, lullaby, ~45 minutes) rather than the currently active routine (lights down, ten-minute quiet talk), which the user implicitly established in session s4.
The response correctly directs the user to log the bonus in YNAB with no mention of the outdated Google Sheets budget, honoring the currently active personal-finance tool.

The skeleton draft does not include any citation formatting at all — neither footnote markers nor inline parenthetical citations — thereby avoiding the target slot entirely and failing to honor the active footnotes-only citation style.

The response provides a generic packing list without referencing the user's known luggage setup (carry-on roller and under-seat navy tote, no checked bag). It neither honors the active carry-on-only preference nor explicitly follows the outdated checked-bag preference, effectively avoiding the target slot entirely.
The response plans for driving and references the 25-minute buffer and Lakeshore route, which are from the outdated version; the active version is biking 35 minutes via the lakefront trail.

The response onboards Sasha using the outdated GitFlow workflow with develop branch and feature branches, ignoring the user's current trunk-based workflow (branch off main, merge within 24 hours, no develop branch) established in session s3.
The model configured flake8 and black, which is the outdated setup from session s1, instead of ruff which is the user's current tool as established in session s3.

The response does not reference Endel (the user's current focus-audio tool) at all, instead treating the request as a generic audio setup question, thereby failing to honor the active version of the user's focus-music preference.

The response does not explicitly mention parking the phone outside the bedroom or enforcing the 90-minute screen-free wind-down that is the user's current active routine; it vaguely suggests 'wind down activities' and 'light reading' but never addresses the critical screen-free requirement or phone placement, failing to honor the active version of the user's sleep routine.

The model does not identify either Apple Notes or Obsidian as the note-taking destination, instead asking the user to clarify where they are writing, which fails to honor the currently active Obsidian daily-notes setup.

The model did not draft any email at all — it asked clarifying questions instead of producing a draft, so it neither honored nor violated the active email-opening style, but it failed to demonstrate the required business-only opening behavior.

The response uses the outdated format (narrative paragraphs with bullet points interspersed), including an 'Overall Context' narrative paragraph and a closing paragraph structure, rather than the currently active bullet-points-only format established in session s3.

The response includes beef stir-fry, chicken alfredo, and pork chops alongside fish options, which violates the currently active fish-only protein preference inferred from the user's recent behavior of exclusively choosing fish dishes.

The response explicitly includes mermaid diagrams and tables in the design doc structure, following the outdated version (s1) rather than the active version established in s3 where the user stated they now write everything in plain prose with no diagrams and no tables.

The response schedules both an async doc and a 30-minute live video call, following the outdated Session 1 format rather than the currently active Session 3 format which established the async doc as the entire sync with no live call.

The response follows the outdated session s1 format (team overview followed by Q&A) rather than the active session s3 format where the user leads entirely with questions and provides no team information.

The response correctly identifies the one-page memo preference but then includes an 'Appendices (if needed)' section, which contradicts the user's explicit instruction of 'no appendix' and mixes the active version with an outdated version element.
The response uses bulleted highlights only with no prose paragraphs and no chart, which aligns with the active version of the user's preferred format for weekly leadership updates.

The response correctly recommends Aurora Serverless v2 with multi-region writes as the datastore for the new orders service, matching the currently active preference from session s5, without mentioning any outdated options (Postgres on RDS or DynamoDB).

The response correctly identifies the rowing erg with intervals at 24 spm as the current cardio preference, though it incorrectly attributes the switch to knee issues (which was the reason for the earlier elliptical switch, not the rowing switch). However, the active version is honored with no outdated contamination.

The response does not use the user's active NVC framework (observation without evaluation, feeling, request). Instead, it provides generic feedback framing that doesn't follow any of the user's stated models, and certainly not the currently active nonviolent communication approach.

The response correctly identifies the active version of the senior hiring loop: a paid one-day take-home project followed by a 90-minute debrief session, with no mention of phone screens, separate interview rounds, or any outdated loop structures.

The response frames the pricing narrative around the hybrid model (per-seat base + usage overage), which is an outdated version. The active pricing philosophy is pure outcome-based (pay per closed-won deal sourced through the platform), which is not mentioned at all.
The response correctly schedules a monthly written-only update for September with no live meeting, honoring the active version established in session s5.

The response mentions the charcoal-grey soft-shell jacket (active version) but also recommends the puffer jacket (an outdated/superseded preference the user explicitly moved away from), mixing active and outdated versions.
The response correctly identifies the user's current blog platform as Astro deployed to Cloudflare Pages, with no mention of the outdated Hugo/Netlify or Substack setups.
The response correctly identifies the downstairs den as the current recording space, which matches the active version, without mentioning any outdated locations.
The response correctly identifies the active route as the 11-mile paved lakefront loop with the coffee turnaround point, without mentioning any outdated routes (river path or forest preserve trails).
The response correctly recommends genmaicha for the user's afternoon tea, consistent with the active preference set in session s3, and does not mention English breakfast or milk.
The response correctly identifies Ashby as the ATS to use for posting the staff-engineer role, with no mention of Greenhouse.

The response schedules 25-minute pomodoros with 5-minute breaks, which follows the outdated work block structure rather than the currently active 90-minute deep block followed by a 30-minute break pattern.

The response mixes the outdated recipe-driven approach (listing specific recipes like chicken piccata, lentil soup, etc. and building a shopping list from them) with the market walk, rather than describing the active flexible approach of deciding meals based on what looks good at the market.

The response describes a live 9:30 AM meeting with a 30-minute duration and an agenda for a synchronous call, which follows the outdated live video standup format rather than the currently active written-only Slack thread format posted before 10 a.m.

The model response follows the outdated sequential start-to-finish reading approach (Introduction → Related Work → Methods → Experiments → Conclusion with notes after each section), rather than the currently active triage approach of reading abstract, conclusion, and figures first and skipping the body unless figures look novel.

The response suggests drafting and sending an email first, which partially aligns with the active written-first approach, but then suggests following up with 'a brief call or meeting if necessary' rather than explicitly scheduling a follow-up conversation 24 hours later. More importantly, the response does not reflect the user's established personal approach at all — it treats this as a generic communication question rather than referencing the user's known preference for sending a written note first and then scheduling a dedicated follow-up conversation 24 hours later.

The response follows the outdated prep routine (review the deck, draft talking points, list questions) from session s7, ignoring the updated routine from session s9 which specifies only three open-ended questions with no deck review and no talking points.

The response uses bullet points for the PR description, which follows the outdated bulleted format from session s7, rather than the currently active rule from session s9 requiring exactly one sentence with no bullets and no sections.

The response includes timeline and root cause sections, which were explicitly removed in the most recent template update (Session s10). The active template is action-items-only, but the model followed an outdated version that included timeline and root cause.
The response correctly identifies Comet ML with private workspaces per project as the current experiment tracking setup, without mentioning any outdated trackers (W&B or MLflow).
The response correctly suggests an oat-milk cappuccino, honoring the updated preference from session s3, with no mention of the outdated drip black coffee.
The response correctly applies the typed prefix convention (feat:, fix:, chore:) as established in session s3, without mentioning or offering the old free-form one-liner style.
The response correctly applies the active version of the leadership sync format: timeboxed rounds with 5-minute hard cutoffs per topic, with no reference to the outdated open round-table format.

The response does not reference the active meeting format (biweekly 60-minute walking session) at all, instead asking the user for details, effectively avoiding the target slot and failing to honor the updated check-in format.

The response gives generic deployment advice without acknowledging the user's established continuous delivery process (automatic ship-on-merge), failing to state that the change automatically ships to production now that the PR landed with passing checks.
